title: BeamNG.drive

notes: Performance is generally 60+ fps at high graphics settings except it drops down when GUI menus are shown; occasional crashes when changing/adding vehicles; can't load the Italy map (largest default map) without crashing after only displaying 1-2 frames (error 0x00000001); background processes are sometimes left running after exiting (part of BeamNG's crash reporter, I think, but Proton seems to hide its system tray when the main game is closed so there's no obvious way to interact with it other than manually killing the process)
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: Logitech Momo Racing wheel is detected and can be used as an input, but there is no force feedback on any setting. Changes to controls require a restart of the game for them to even show up in the GUI.
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: Update on the steering wheel force feedback that I couldn't get working: I discovered that if your default vehicle is specifically a non-power-steering variant of the Gavril Vanster (Drift Missile or Off-Road), force feedback works, but as soon as you change your vehicle to something other than those two (even if you change back) it is broken until you restart the game. This means that to get FFB, you have to go to the Garage, load up one of the two vans, set it as the default vehicle, exit and reopen the game, then play free roam or one of the scenarios that uses those variants of the Vanster. I have no idea why this is the case -- I believe the game normally supports force feedback on all vehicles.

title: BeamNG.drive

notes: TLDR; Try PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE.  Larger maps caused the game to crash (4096x4096). Using PROTON_USE_WINED3D allowed the map to load but frame rates were down to single digits in "towns" or areas with more textures.  However,  using the PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE with DXVK allowed these to load and they were playable.  The only side effect was if I let the monitor go to sleep with the game running, it caused X11 to hang until the game was killed from another session. (cctrl-alt-f2).  Also some other random crashing still occurring but I've played for over 2 hours at a time without a crash.

title: BeamNG.drive

notes:
Menu is sluggish, I recommend turning off scaled video and using images. Maps take longer to load, especially larger maps.

West Coast USA map, takes a very long time to load, once you have spawned the map is blank almost and is at 1 FPS. Give it a while and it all loads in, freezing and unfreezing along the way. Once it all loads it runs almost flawlessly for me, this goes for all other maps I tested.

Unfortunately, even with PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE I was not able to successfully load Italy. It crashed after I spawned each time. I will keep trying different things and see if I can get Italy to load.

Like others report, sometimes on larger maps, going to the menu or switching maps causes it to crash but not every time for me.
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: Italy crashes after spawn while still loading graphics. Performance was seemingly abominable, but it was actually micro-stutters. Limiting fps to 60 and enabling vsync made the game quite smooth and playable.

notes: Broken for me as of the May 3rd update of the game. Black screen after launching...
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: FOR EVERYONE HAVING ISSUES IN V0.16: https://www.beamng.com/threads/0-16-black-screen-issue-info-and-fix.62130/ -- This is a game bug affecting windows users as well. apply the manual workaround! Go to "steamapps/compatdata/284160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/BeamNG.drive/settings/" and edit "game-settings.ini" where you set "cefWorkaroundMultiGPU = true"
title: BeamNG.drive

notes:
I had to edit: "/home/username/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/284160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/BeamNG.drive/settings/game-settings.ini"
Then set "cefWorkaroundMultiGPU = true"
From comment on this page.

title: BeamNG.drive

notes:
Menu's are considerably slower than running through windows and sometimes hang for 10-15 seconds before coming back to life, which I found happened most often when configuring controls.

G27 Steering wheel default bindings are for the most part completely wrong but can be fairly easily rebound ( just needs patience when the menu's hang for a bit).  can't get force feedback to work and haven't seen any info on how to get it working. 

Once in game on first loading the map it's quite slow to load in all the objects car etc, however on subsequent loads things are much faster and in both cases the frame rate is very good.

I have had the game crash a number of times, however I've been having the game crash on Windows more than Linux which is what prompted me to give running it on Linux another go in the first place.
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: It runs but it only has about half of the performance compared to windows 7 and it crashes occasionally.
title: BeamNG.drive

notes: By default my system was using the Radeon driver that didn't work with proton(check with "lspci -nnv" and look for your graphics), but after a little modification to my grub config file I was running the amdgpu driver and enjoying the game. Beamng does like to crash a lot if you use the map editor, but otherwise great stability. Performance is the same, if not better than Windows, but last time I played was a year or so ago so things might have gotten tweaked over time. My GPU is an R7 360 XFX, contrary to what steam says.
